qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated Browser Control API reaches localhost-only dashboard config and exposes the Parse master key
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: GHSA-cvwj-6c9h-jg6v is the agent-endpoint authorization flaw on `POST /apps/:appId/agent`: upstream `9.0.0-alpha.8` adds per-app access checks and read-only master-key enforcement in `Parse-Dashboard/app.js`. This finding instead abuses unauthenticated `/browser-control/*` endpoints to drive a server-side browser to `/parse-dashboard-config.json` and exfiltrate the dashboard master key, so the vulnerable code path and missing guard are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final `qualifying_vuln` finding identifies the advisory-described root cause on the AI agent endpoint. The only qualifying finding is a separate Browser Control access-control bypass that leaks the dashboard config through a localhost browser pivot rather than missing authorization on `POST /apps/:appId/agent`.
evidence: The advisory describes missing authorization on `POST /apps/:appId/agent`, including cross-app access for authenticated users and write-capability escalation for read-only users.
The session manifest lists exactly one qualifying finding: `browser_control_localhost_config_bypass`.

The retained Browser Control report and PoC evidence describe unauthenticated `/browser-control/*` access that navigates to `/parse-dashboard-config.json` and reads `masterKey`, not the advisory's agent route.

Upstream `9.0.0-alpha.8` changes only `Parse-Dashboard/app.js`, adds per-app authorization checks for the agent handler, swaps read-only users to `readOnlyMasterKey`, and strips client-supplied write permissions; it does not patch Browser Control files.
